status: RUN 2026-07-04. BSBM 200M (pc=570000) throughput matrix on the m7a.4xlarge box, Fluree v4.1.2 (25d8c28f4). Real BSBM: bsbmtools-0.2 test driver (randomized query mix -> QMpH). Explore + BusinessIntelligence (update omitted at scale) x {1,4,8,16,32} clients, 0 timeouts.

dataset.name: BSBM 200M (pc=570000, forward-chained)
dataset.description: Berlin SPARQL Benchmark e-commerce dataset, 200M stress scale point. Deterministic generator (no seed); pin = toolkit SHA + -pc + flags.

methodology.notes: BI run single-client only (multi-client BI@200M would be hours and is dominated by the -fc root-type artifact). NOTE: in the unattended full-suite run the BI@200M c1 cell OOM-killed the server (heavy root-type BI query working set + ~15 GiB already-filled LeafletCache exceeded the 64 GB box). Re-running it from a freshly-restarted server (cache headroom available) completed cleanly: QMpH 12.28, 1 timeout (Q4). The committed bi__c1.xml is that clean re-run. Headroom, not a hard regression.

fluree.label: Fluree
fluree.install: Built from source, fluree/db branch feature/warm-on-write-reindex-cache (rebased with fix/filtered-delete-staging-hang). Binary reports 'fluree 4.1.1' (pre-release build of v4.1.2).
fluree.config: default server config; LeafletCache budget ~21.5 GiB

caveats: Real BSBM via bsbmtools-0.2 (randomized query mix, QMpH).

BI@200M single-client can exceed 64 GB RAM when a root-type (ProductType1) analytic query lands on top of a filled leaflet cache -> server OOM. Reproducible workaround: more RAM, smaller cache budget, or exclude the root type from the param pool / regenerate without -fc.
Absolute QMpH is comparable engine-vs-engine on this box, not across toolkit versions/scales.
200M explore re-measured on the warmed ledger (a warm-up pass then w3 r30); run-to-run variance on the cold 49GB ledger is ~7% (1M/100M explore are flat vs the prior v4.1.1/warm-on-write build).
